pr.title: label most math.s functions from Pikmin map
pr.author: EpochFlame

summary: Labeled a large cluster of MSL math/trig assembly symbols using the Pikmin map, split the original asm/MSL/math.s into separate math_1.s and trigf.s units, and propagated the newly named trig calls across many asm files. The main unidentified trig internals func_80326240 and func_803263D4 became cosf and sinf, wrapper-style symbols became cos__Ff and sin__Ff, func_803261B4 became fabsf__Ff, and trig data tables were renamed to descriptive names such as __sincos_on_quadrant, __sincos_poly, tmp_float, and __four_over_pi_m1. The author noted uncertainty about whether lbl_80400770 and lbl_80400774 belonged in trigf.s, while saying the global data order was correct.

key_files.role: Original MSL math assembly file; many trig-related functions and data were removed into split files, and rodata labels __sincos_on_quadrant and __sincos_poly were named.
New split MSL math file containing frexp and fabsf__Ff.
New split MSL trig file containing tanf, cos__Ff, sin__Ff, cosf, sinf, __sinit_trigf_c, tmp_float, lbl_80400770, lbl_80400774, and __four_over_pi_m1.
Build object list updated to include asm/MSL/math_1.o and asm/MSL/trigf.o before asm/MSL/math.o.
Representative high-volume callsite relabeling from anonymous trig functions to cosf/sinf.
Representative high-volume item-system callsite relabeling from anonymous trig functions to cosf/sinf.
Representative baselib callsite relabeling from anonymous trig functions to cosf/sinf.

what_changed: Split MSL math assembly further by moving frexp into new asm/MSL/math_1.s and moving trig-related code into new asm/MSL/trigf.s.
Added asm/MSL/math_1.o and asm/MSL/trigf.o to obj_files.mk before asm/MSL/math.o, preserving object ordering around the split.
Renamed func_80326240 to cosf and func_803263D4 to sinf.
Renamed wrapper symbols func_80326200 and func_80326220 to cos__Ff and sin__Ff.
Relabeled tanf internals so its calls now branch to cos__Ff and sin__Ff rather than anonymous func_80326200/func_80326220.
Relabeled many external callsites across camera, fighter, item, stage, text, baselib, and Dolphin asm files from func_80326240/func_803263D4 to cosf/sinf.
Renamed rodata tables lbl_803B9358 and lbl_803B9378 to __sincos_on_quadrant and __sincos_poly.
Moved/renamed trig-related data including tmp_float and __four_over_pi_m1 into trigf.s.
smart_moves: Used the Pikmin map as external evidence to replace anonymous address-based function labels with library-style MSL trig names.
Did not only label function definitions; the PR also updated every visible callsite, making future grep/search results reflect semantic function names across the codebase.
Preserved object order by adding math_1.o and trigf.o immediately before math.o in obj_files.mk, an important tactic when splitting asm while keeping link layout stable.
Separated frexp and trigf code into smaller assembly units, reducing the size and ambiguity of asm/MSL/math.s for later decompilation.
Named supporting trig tables, not just functions, which makes later sin/cos polynomial reconstruction easier.
Explicitly called out uncertainty about two global data labels instead of overclaiming their placement.
decomp_lessons: When a known SDK/library map is available, use it to label both definitions and all downstream branch targets; this turns anonymous call graphs into searchable semantic APIs.
File splits in assembly must be paired with object-list updates that preserve link order.
For math-library decompilation, table labels such as quadrant tables and polynomial coefficient tables are often as useful as function labels.
Wrapper symbols can coexist with core C/library symbols: this PR distinguished cos__Ff/sin__Ff wrappers from cosf/sinf implementations.
If data placement is uncertain, document the uncertainty and preserve proven ordering rather than inventing stronger claims.
A broad callsite relabeling pass can touch many unrelated gameplay systems without changing behavior; classify such PRs as symbol/name hygiene rather than gameplay changes.

naming_conventions.pattern: MSL single-precision trig implementations use standard names cosf and sinf.
Some float wrapper functions use C++-mangled-style suffixes in asm labels.
Internal trig data tables use double-underscore descriptive names.
Uncertain or still-unknown globals may remain as address labels even after adjacent known data is named.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split asm/MSL/math.s into math_1.s and trigf.s while adding corresponding objects to obj_files.mk to maintain link order.
Replaced branch targets in asm comments and labels from func_80326240/func_803263D4 to cosf/sinf across many object files without altering instruction bytes.
Renamed table references from lbl_803B9358/lbl_803B9378 to __sincos_on_quadrant/__sincos_poly while preserving the same addresses and data contents.
Kept global data order intact despite uncertainty about specific labels, according to the author's note.
Moved trig initializer code into trigf.s and updated the init table entry to __sinit_trigf_c.

review_feedback.text: Author said the PR also split files further and was not sure about two global data labels, but believed the order was correct.
Author specifically noted uncertainty about whether lbl_80400770 and lbl_80400774 should be in trigf.s.
RevoSucks approved the PR with no written review comments.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for whether lbl_80400770 and lbl_80400774 were confirmed, renamed, or moved out of trigf.s.
Search later decomp work for source equivalents of asm/MSL/trigf.s, especially cosf, sinf, tanf, __sincos_poly, and __four_over_pi_m1.
Look for additional Pikmin map based labeling passes in MSL or Dolphin libraries.
Verify whether cos__Ff and sin__Ff wrappers remain as asm labels or are later represented as C/C++ declarations.
